title: rhamnus alpina var. glaucophylla
intro: Rhamnus alpina var. glaucophylla, also known as the Alpine Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its edible berries and medicinal properties. The plant is used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as digestive issues, diarrhea, and skin irritations due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rhamnus alpina var. glaucophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves, bark, and berries, and are commonly used in traditional European folk medicine.
